name: Bellerophon
description: Bellerophon, a hero of Greek mythology, was famed for his courage and skill, particularly his ability to tame the winged horse Pegasus. Born in Corinth, his early life was marred by tragedy, including the accidental killing of his brother, which led to his exile. He sought purification from King Proetus of Tiryns, but the king's wife, Stheneboea (or Anteia), falsely accused Bellerophon of attempting to seduce her. Enraged, Proetus sent Bellerophon to King Iobates of Lycia with a sealed message instructing Iobates to kill him. However, bound by the laws of hospitality, Iobates instead tasked Bellerophon with a series of dangerous feats, hoping he would perish. Bellerophon successfully completed these tasks, including slaying the Chimera, a fire-breathing monster with the head of a lion, the body of a goat, and the tail of a serpent. He also fought against the Amazons and defeated Lycian pirates. Impressed by Bellerophon's prowess, Iobates revealed the contents of Proetus' letter and offered Bellerophon his daughter, Philonoe, in marriage, granting him half his kingdom. Bellerophon's hubris eventually led to his downfall. He attempted to fly Pegasus to Mount Olympus to join the gods, an act of excessive pride that angered Zeus. Zeus sent a gadfly to sting Pegasus, causing Bellerophon to fall back to Earth. He survived, but spent the rest of his life wandering alone, crippled and despised by the gods, a cautionary tale against hubris and overreaching ambition. His story highlights themes of exile, betrayal, divine favor, and the consequences of pride.
subtitle: Tamer of Pegasus, Slayer of Chimera
achievements: Marrying Philonoe and gaining a kingdom

keyMyths.title: The Taming of Pegasus
keyMyths.description: Bellerophon, guided by the seer Polyeidos, slept in the temple of Athena, who provided him with a golden bridle. He used this bridle to tame the winged horse Pegasus, allowing him to accomplish great feats.

keyMyths.title: The Slaying of the Chimera
keyMyths.description: King Iobates tasked Bellerophon with killing the Chimera, a fearsome monster. Riding Pegasus, Bellerophon soared above the Chimera and eventually killed it, either by piercing it with his spear or by melting its throat with a lump of lead attached to his spear.

keyMyths.title: Bellerophon's Fall
keyMyths.description: Bellerophon, in his hubris, attempted to fly Pegasus to Mount Olympus. Zeus, angered by this arrogance, sent a gadfly to sting Pegasus, causing Bellerophon to fall to Earth, where he lived out his days as a wanderer.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: Provides early accounts of Bellerophon's exploits, including the slaying of the Chimera.
sources.text: Provides early accounts of Bellerophon's exploits, including the slaying of the Chimera.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: Mentions Pegasus and Bellerophon's connection to the winged horse.
sources.text: Mentions Pegasus and Bellerophon's connection to the winged horse.

sources.author: Pindar
sources.description: Details Bellerophon's attempt to reach Olympus and his subsequent fall.
